title: How to Play Minesweeper
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2lcb2","text":"Minesweeper is a classic single-player puzzle game that is very fun to play. Let's begin!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Choose a difficulty level according to your experience and preference: Beginner, Intermediate, Expert, or Custom.
Launch the Minesweeper application or online game on your computer or mobile device. My favorite website for playing Minesweeper is https://minesweeper.online/ .
Left-click on any cell to start the game. You will not encounter a mine at the initial click.
On some versions, you can use hints when unsure of the next cell to open.
The board is divided into cells, with mines randomly distributed. To win, you need to open all the cells. Cells suspected of being mines can be marked with a flag using the right-click.
After a game, you can see many statistics such as time, clicks, efficiency and win streak. Try to beat yourself!
You can start a new game by clicking the smiley face (or the sad face if you just lost) on top of the board. Have fun!

Keep clicking to try to reveal more safe cells based on the clue you get from opened cells. After clicking a cell, you'll see one of three things: A number: This number indicates the count of mines adjacent to that cell. An empty cell: If the clicked cell has no adjacent mines, the game will automatically reveal adjacent empty cells until it reaches cells with numbers. A mine: If you reveal a mine, the game is over, and you lose.
creator: Cheng Zhang
